chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-intarea

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: Is the audio ok for remote ?

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: Tobias

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: wrong msg

chat.author: Tobias Fiebig
chat.text: What message? I did not say anything?

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: I type it by mistake in the wrong windows (and it was not even about you ;-) )

chat.author: Tobias Fiebig
chat.text: ah

chat.author: Tobias Fiebig
chat.text: also a dedicated number ensures overlap free numbering vs. e.g. using rfc1918 addr for this

chat.author: Maria Matějka
chat.text: The dedicated number signals the intent on all layers. We've seen this intent signaling already by operators squatting on 1.1.1.1; no more these please. The allocation is perfectly justified.

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: Remco's draft should use TBD though rather than guessing 192.0.0.11 will be used

chat.author: Remco van Mook
chat.text: TBD is fair pushback - but that made it even more abstract. Having the number in made it readable imho. Happy to take guidance how this is done for special purpose registry, I couldn't really find anything

chat.author: Behcet Sarikaya
chat.text: @Eric: audio for remote does not seem to work today

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: @Behcet is that still a problem?

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: or a temp issue

chat.author: Behcet Sarikaya
chat.text: @Eric: Nver mind, I rebooted and it works now :+1:

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: glad to hear it!

chat.author: Behcet Sarikaya
chat.text: @Tommy: yes, thanks!

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: I would have guessed battery

chat.author: Ondřej Caletka
chat.text: Isn't the whole issue here that most fixed  networks don't do proper policing on the upstream path before BNG, so if the sum of upstream traffic from customers goes over the capacity of the OLT-BNG link, bad things can happen?

chat.author: Ondřej Caletka
chat.text: Oh, they just said it :)

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: Curious to see the overlap with https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6169

chat.author: Carlos Bernardos
chat.text: good point @Eric

chat.author: Carlos Bernardos
chat.text: I think many IETF protocols already incorporate some of the mechanisms proposed here, for example Mobile IPv6 tunneling (RFC 6275) specify the validation of the tunnel headers
